title: [phase:critique] [family:backend-fit] [provider_hint:codex] Return one concise line with your initial critique of using Google AI agent tooling as the main backend for Cursorvers.Inc. Findings first, one or two sentences only.
summary_text: Good direction, but it only closes part of the drift and can introduce new coupling if done naively. - Biggest risk: if `webhook-utils` imports the barrel (`../_shared/cursorvers.ts`), it will pull facade/orchestrator paths and their env-sensitive deps; use contracts-only imports (e.g., `cursorvers-wrapper-contracts.ts`) to keep utils pure ([cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts), [supabase.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/supabase.ts)). - Parsing move is low-risk and should reduce drift because parser helpers already exist and are tested ([webhook-utils.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils.ts), [webhook-utils_test.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils_test.ts)); update `line-webhook` to consume them in the explicit prefix branch. - Limit-alignment move is correct if kept deterministic: compute `min(MAX_INPUT_LENGTH, wrapper_limit)` in one utility and add edge handling for invalid env values. - Minimum missing tests before calling it done: index-level dispatch tests for `polish:`/`check:` routing + blank input behavior + boundary length checks in `line-webhook` (current tests are mostly util/lib-level, not call-site). - Remaining drift not solved by this plan: optional `formattedMessage` contract handling in risk path and duplicated keyword detection logic still exist outside parsing/limits scope.
